kills: C1's only abstract-existence evidence is provably empty: 'clamp + one pendant edge = pattern-flipper' cannot work. A pendant vertex u always has >= 3 colors available distinct from any target vertex (5 colors minus its one neighbor minus the target), so no quadruple involving u can have (diff,diff) infeasible; quadruples not involving u reduce to the clamp graph alone, so the pendant adds nothing. The claim confuses the L56 splice lemma (forced-same + pendant -> clamp) with a nonexistent converse. The graded slice C1 needs is therefore not established even abstractly by anything in the proposal.

C3 Claim 1 is structurally broken: proper colorings are discrete assignments and do not transport along flexes, so off the walls the 'correspondence-valued local system' is the CONSTANT system with trivial monodromy. All real content (pinned shared vertices, wall coincidence edges, deck permutation sigma) is exactly C1's finite transfer algebra; the sheaf/wall-crossing layer is decoration. The stated trace EQUALITY is also false (0/1 relation vs multiplicity-weighted matrix; cross-copy coincidences and identifications shrink the true coloring set); only the zero-trace one-way blade survives, which the proposal itself half-concedes in C3's caveat.

The sole calibration (Moser spindle at k=3) exercises ONLY L55 route (i): the braced rhombus realizes the forced-same relation I through width-1 ports, composed with one J-I edge. That is the route the frontier declares dead at k=5 (L56/L57). The proposal exhibits zero working instances of its actual mechanism (graded width >= 2 transfer) at any k >= 3 in any normed plane; the only true instance of the 'flux quantization' schema anywhere is k=2 odd cycles.

Internal contradiction: first_principles asserts 'The lineage cannot contain this... retrodicting L57's total freeness', but L57 is pair-level only and silent on quadruples; C2 and the experiment exist precisely because flippers could still hide in the lineage. Also 'all 12 exhaustively dead' overstates L57, which records 874 at ~96% complete.

The experiment as specified cannot decide C2: merge-only conditioning detects quadruples whose infeasible cell involves a 'same' pattern (directly or via the symmetric conditioning pair) but is structurally blind to quadruples whose ONLY infeasible cell is (diff,diff). Deciding C2 requires the dual edge-conditioned sweep too (forced_same infrastructure already exists in shotC/shotD; ~2x cost). 'Zero hits proves holonomy material must be built outside the lineage' is a further overclaim: S199 is one of 12 graphs and m=2 interfaces are the only width tested.

C1 does not weaken the W3 wall; it reshapes and partially heightens it: the graded slice must be a NEW chi-5 UDG outside the lineage (per L57), realizable with the two interfaces EXACTLY congruent under a finite-order rotation with p not dividing N+s (an extra exact-closure Diophantine constraint), and the repo has no tool to certify realizability at the proposed ~150-vtx scale (Groebner dead beyond ~14 vtx; L52 kills DOF heuristics). The proposal is silent on the certification step.

No-buffer violations: chi=5 slice at <= 150 vertices (smaller than every known chi-5 UDG; repo lineage floor is 199), chi >= 6 certificate under 1000 vertices (de Grey needed 1581 for chi >= 5), and r <= 4 walls are unsupported numerology.

Novelty overclaim: tr(T^N P_sigma) for twisted cyclic closures is the standard twisted-boundary-condition antiferromagnetic Potts / chromatic-polynomial transfer matrix (Beraha-Kahane-Weiss; Salas-Sokal cyclic and Mobius strips). What is new is only the coupling to UDG realizability and rotational closure; the trace certificate itself is classical.

C2's 'structural form' (generic rigidity of the bar framework implies complete pairwise pattern transfer at k=5) has no mechanism: it rests on Kempe chains 'acting transitively', which is unproven for these graphs (max degree far above 5; no Kempe-equivalence result applies). C2 survives only as a falsifiable conjecture with a decisive test, not as a structural law.
prior_art_collisions: Salas-Sokal and Beraha-Kahane-Weiss: twisted/cyclic-strip chromatic-polynomial transfer matrices; tr(T^N P_sigma) and its zero-count consequences are standard statistical-mechanics machinery, contradicting C1's novelty claim for the trace formula itself.

de Grey 2018 / Polymath16 spindling and virtual edges: the Moser 'calibration' in C3 is the classical spindle argument (forced-same I relation through single ports) restated; this is L55 route (i), already dead at k=5 on the lineage.
Exoo-Ismailescu chi >= 5 UDG constructions: rotational orbit closure of congruent gadget copies with SAT verification is their standard production move; the geometric closure step in C1 is not new.

Asimow-Roth / Connelly rigidity theory: realization-space stratification and flex loops are classical; the coloring coupling appears genuinely absent from that literature, but my analysis shows the coupling collapses to finite transfer algebra, so the novel part is the part that does no work.

Kempe equivalence literature (Las Vergnas-Meyniel; Mohar; Bonamy et al.): no result gives Kempe transitivity of 5-colorings for graphs of max degree > 5, so C2's plausibility argument has no published support.

Repo-internal: L55 monoid (width-1 kill, honored by C1), L45/L56/L57 (pair-level freeness; quadruple forcing genuinely untested, so C1/C2 are not DOA), L51 (48-vtx abstract clamp and the C6-closure no-forcing datum), L52 (DOF/over-determination kill, honored).

Woodall 1973 and Chilakamarri: the detector facts the self-check invokes (Q^2 bipartite, L^infty chi=4) are correct and correctly used, though the detector pass is vacuous for any finite-UDG-certificate schema.
result.surviving_kernel: Two pieces survive. (1) Joint-pattern (quadruple) conditional forcing in the lineage is a genuinely untested degree of freedom: L45/L56/L57 only ever tested unary pairs, so a width-2 'flipper' could in principle hide there, and the conditional forcing sweep (run with BOTH merge and edge conditioning) is a sound, cheap, decisive experiment either way. (2) The Z/2-graded width-2 flipper slice is a legitimate strictly-weaker-than-clamp target: it evades the L55 monoid (width 2; the partition-algebra analog of the width-1 relation monoid does contain graded elements), evades all pair-level sweeps (its marginals are free), and converts to a chi >= 6 UDG via the sound odd-N twisted-closure zero-trace blade. Its abstract existence reduces cleanly (my derivation, not the proposal's) to: find a 6-critical graph K with two independent edges e1=(a1,a2), e2=(b1,b2) such that chi((K - e1 - e2)/(a1=a2)/(b1=b2)) >= 6; (diff,diff)-infeasibility is automatic from chi(K)=6 and mixed-cell feasibility from criticality. All Euclidean difficulty remains W3 realizability plus the exact finite-order closure congruence.
result.cheapest_decisive_test: SAT sweep over the ~25k pairs of independent edges (e1,e2) of M^3(C_5) (47 vtx, 236 edges, already in repo as L51's H): for each pair, one Cadical 5-colorability call on (K - e1 - e2) with both endpoint pairs merged; any UNSAT proves the abstract Z/2 flipper slice exists (replacing the broken pendant claim), total runtime minutes on the existing f1pt_lib/sat_kcolor stack. Follow-on (~20-30 min): 2000-pair random conditional-forcing sample on S199 with BOTH merge and edge conditioning, for the first read on C2.
result.steelman: Drop the cosheaf/monodromy layer entirely (it is the constant sheaf; flexes are at most a heuristic for proposing congruent interfaces and wall events). Keep three things. First, the sound certificate: if a chi-5 UDG slice S has disjoint congruent width-m interfaces whose 5-coloring transfer relation is Z/p-graded, then any exact rotational closure with p not dividing N+s is a finite UDG with zero proper 5-colorings, hence chi(R^2) >= 6; soundness needs only the one-way zero-trace blade, which is robust to extra coincidences and identifications. Second, the abstract target, correctly reduced: a 6-critical K minus two independent edges whose double contraction stays 6-chromatic is exactly a p=2 flipper slice; search for it by SAT starting from M^3(C_5) and the h5/h6 abstract graphs. Third, the experiment, repaired: run the conditional forcing sweep on S199 with both merge and edge conditioning (then upward through the lineage), which for the first time tests joint-pattern forcing; zero hits makes C2 a real structural datum on S199 and any hit is the first k=5 forcing object of any kind in the lineage. Everything Euclidean still funnels into W3: realizing the flipper slice and its exact odd-order closure, a target strictly larger than the clamp but behind the same wall.
